patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/2] mm: don't apply task mempolicy to unmovable kernel allocations

version_history.subject: [PATCH 0/2] mm: don't apply task mempolicy to unmovable kernel allocations

series_items.subject: [PATCH 1/2] mm/filemap: place page-cache folios via an explicit mempolicy
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's filemap module to explicitly specify a memory policy for page-cache allocations, rather than relying on the implicit behavior of following the first-touch task's memory policy. This change codifies the existing behavior and ensures that pagecache placement remains stable across future changes. The modification involves adding an explicit call to `folio_alloc_mpol_noprof` in the `filemap_alloc_folio_noprof` function, which takes into account the current task's memory policy for allocation decisions.

series_items.subject: [PATCH 2/2] mm/mempolicy: skip task mempolicy for unmovable unaccounted kernel allocations
series_items.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory policy to skip following a task's memory policy for certain types of unmovable and unaccounted kernel allocations. These allocations, such as those made by interrupt handlers or explicit placement calls, should not be influenced by a task's memory policy. The patch introduces a new helper function, alloc_task_policy(), to identify these allocation types and prevent them from following the task's policy. This change is intended to prevent performance regressions on tiered memory systems where incidental allocations can land on slower memory tiers.

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series prevents a task's NUMA memory policy from influencing incidental kernel allocations that have no direct relationship to the task. Currently, these allocations follow the task's policy, which can lead to poor placement on tiered-memory systems and cause regressions. The fix is to only apply the task policy to movable allocations explicitly tied to the task via __GFP_ACCOUNT, while preferring node-local placement for unmovable kernel allocations. This change ensures that kernel memory is placed in a way that minimizes performance regressions and maintains existing behavior for page cache placement.

review_comments.author: Huang, Ying
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Huang, Ying expressed theoretical agreement but requested empirical evidence of the patch's benefits.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a task's mempolicy is set to MPOL_F_LOCAL, which can lead to incorrect placement of unmovable kernel allocations.
sentiment_signals: patch needs additional handling for MPOL_F_LOCAL policy
review_comments.raw_body:
Personally, I think this should be the right thing to do theoretically.
However, you may need to find some practical issues that this resolves.

---

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: The author acknowledges that there is at least one scenario where the patch improves performance, specifically when BIOS-configured CXL memory is brought up in ZONE_NORMAL and a task interleave policy causes unaccounted kernel allocations to land on the remote node. The author agrees that this is an improvement but notes it's not a complete fix and plans to send a v2 with additional fixes.
review_comments.raw_body:
I don't entirely disagree, but there is at least one scenario where this
is an obvious improvement:

  BIOS-configured CXL memory brought up in ZONE_NORMAL. 

An task interleave policy on such a system will end up with unaccounted
kernel allocations landing on the remote node, which is just not
preferable at all (and uncorrectable).

It's not a complete fix (fallbacks can still occur under pressure), but
it's one piece of the puzzle.


Forward looking:  This patch makes private-node's with ZONE_NORMAL
reliably hot-un-pluggable.  But that improvement is obviously predicated
on work that isn't upstream (yet :] ).

I need to send a v2 of this with SLAB_ACCOUNT fixed up, and some numbers
to justify dropping the pagecache fix.  But I will probably sandbag this
a bit until i finally send out v5 of private nodes.

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Huang, Ying
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that kernel-allocated memory in ZONE_NORMAL and ZONE_MOVABLE may not follow the task's mempolicy, as these zones are not necessarily tied to the task

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a task's mempolicy is set to MPOL_F_LOCAL, which would cause unmovable kernel allocations to still follow the task policy.
sentiment_signals: patch needs additional handling for MPOL_F_LOCAL
review_comments.raw_body:
Per my understanding, kernel configured CXL memory can be in ZONE_NORMAL
and BIOS-configured one can be in ZONE_MOVABLE.  Right?

---

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying
